pattern: An island in the second grid counts only if every one of its cells is also land in the first grid, checked during a single flood fill.
hints: Find each connected group of land in the second grid with a flood fill.
As you flood an island, watch whether any cell sits on water in the first grid.
An island qualifies as a sub-island exactly when all of its cells are land in grid1; count those.

edge_inputs.description: island in grid2 partially over water in grid1

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(120, size))
    g1 = [[1 if rng.random() < 0.6 else 0 for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    g2 = [[1 if rng.random() < 0.5 else 0 for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [g1, g2]
